claim: The Syriac temporal adverb kaddīn (skeleton kdy) appears 1,326 times (3.8% of corpus) while the Jewish Babylonian Aramaic equivalent kəḏēn (skeleton kdn) appears 178 times — an 88:12 ratio supporting primarily Syriac text with minor JBA influence.
evidence_for: Direct skeleton-level claim, testable from corpus
1,326 tokens is a large, statistically stable count
kdy and kdn are distinguishable skeletons
evidence_against: 3.8% is unusually high for a temporal adverb — may indicate non-linguistic function
Could also be some other Semitic word with same skeleton
test: Count skeleton tokens 'kdy' (chedy/chody/ckhedy etc. by char map) and 'kdn' in corpus
test_threshold: kdy:kdn >=4:1 AND kdy >=3% of corpus: confirmed. Otherwise: challenged.
tests_remaining: Count skeleton tokens 'kdy' (chedy/chody/ckhedy etc. by char map) and 'kdn' in corpus
source: Brady Defibaugh (April 2026), A Syriac Pharmaceutical Dispensatory Encoded in the Voynich Manuscript (DANI pipeline v31)
